mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns do not significantly exceed baseline during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above 0.4pp, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving sustained positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of low inflation signals healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, generating positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth during low inflation regimes yields negative or flat 20-day forward returns, or the mechanism reverses direction when CPIAUCSL exceeds 0.3% monthly growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the federal funds rate above 5.0% triggers immediate portfolio rebalancing away from equities toward fixed income, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the threshold effect disappears after adjusting for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting corporate earnings growth and positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE produces a Rank IC below 0.03 for 60-day returns, or the predictive relationship breaks down during supply-constrained periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly correlates with corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns (p > 0.10) over any 5-year rolling window, or the signal consistently underperforms during financial stress episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of high inflation indicates stagflationary pressure, forcing immediate equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during high inflation regimes fails to predict negative 5-day returns, or the mechanism yields positive returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 0.4% monthly growth.
